name: Electric Razor
description: Electric razors and shavers are permitted in both carry-on and checked baggage. They pose no security concern as their blades are enclosed and non-removable.
tsaGuideline: Electric razors are allowed in both carry-on and checked bags. There are no restrictions on electric shavers, trimmers, or grooming devices with enclosed blades.
iataGuideline: Electric razors are permitted in cabin and hold baggage. Battery-powered razors with lithium batteries should follow standard lithium battery guidelines (under 100Wh).
restrictions: Battery-powered razors with lithium batteries should follow lithium battery guidelines
Spare lithium batteries for the razor should be carried in carry-on, not checked
Razors with removable straight blades follow different rules (blades prohibited in carry-on)

faqs.question: Can I pack an electric razor in my carry-on bag?
faqs.answer: Yes. Electric razors, shavers, and trimmers are fully permitted in carry-on baggage. Their blades are enclosed and do not pose a security concern.

faqs.question: What about straight razors or safety razor blades?
faqs.answer: Electric razors are different from straight razors or safety razor blades. Disposable razors and their cartridges are allowed in carry-on, but loose safety razor blades and straight razors must go in checked baggage.

faqs.question: Do I need to remove my electric razor at the security checkpoint?
faqs.answer: No. Electric razors do not need to be removed from your bag during screening, unlike laptops or large electronics. However, a TSA officer may ask to inspect it if the X-ray image is unclear.
